transcript_snippet: We're basically about to solve the greatest detective story ever told... the clues are hidden inside you.
narrative_beat: Cold open — two hosts hook the audience with the premise: a 7-million-year cold case. Tone is conspiratorial and urgent. The big reveal lands: every person on earth is African.
lyria_prompt: Tense cinematic thriller opening. Low drone strings, sparse piano stabs, subtle African percussion (talking drum undertone), building slowly. 60 BPM. Dark and mysterious with a sense of ancient time. No melody — just mood and pulse. No brass fanfares, no major-key brightness, no orchestral swells.

transcript_snippet: The proof is written in tiny accidental spelling mistakes — mutations piling up in our DNA for millions of years.
narrative_beat: The forensic tool is introduced — DNA as evidence. The molecular clock concept arrives. Feeling of a detective piecing clues together, scanning data, finding patterns.
lyria_prompt: Minimalist science-thriller underscore. Sparse electronic textures over plucked strings (pizzicato cello). Steady ticking or clock-like rhythm, 72 BPM. Slightly eerie, like a lab at night. Evokes scanning through data, precision, slow revelation. No vocals. No loud percussion. No cinematic climax.

transcript_snippet: The clock isn't a Swiss watch — scientists calibrate it with anchor points from the fossil record.
narrative_beat: The DNA clock's precision and limitations are explained. Scientists go hunting for a ghost ancestor. Tension builds toward the 7-million-year number.
lyria_prompt: Slow-burn documentary tension. Sustained string pad (low viola, cello) with a subtle rhythmic pulse like a heartbeat at 65 BPM. Gradual harmonic movement. Feels like a countdown. Hint of ancient Africa — kalimba note occasionally breaking through. No resolution. Ends on an unresolved harmonic suspension.

transcript_snippet: In 2001, in the Djurab desert in Chad, they found something that changed everything. A skull.
narrative_beat: The ghost ancestor is found — Toumaï. Brunet's announcement. The foramen magnum smoking gun. This is the biggest revelation of the episode: physical proof of the DNA prediction.
lyria_prompt: Ancient mystery revealed. Deep African percussion (djembe, frame drum) enters slowly under droning strings. A single high woodwind melody (bamboo flute or duduk) emerges — haunting and ancient. 55 BPM, rubato feel. The music should feel like unearthing something sacred. Big but restrained — no triumphant orchestra. No western pop rhythm. No major chord resolution.

transcript_snippet: Lucy was found in Ethiopia in '74 and became an instant icon... every single link in that chain was found in Africa.
narrative_beat: The family album unfolds — Lucy, Homo habilis, an unbroken chain of African fossil evidence. Sense of wonder, accumulation of proof, the story gaining momentum.
lyria_prompt: Warm archaeological wonder. Layered acoustic textures — kora (West African harp), light marimba, sustained strings. 70 BPM, steady and forward-moving. Melodic but subtle — not emotional resolution yet, still building. Evokes savanna and deep time. No drama or climax. No electric instruments. Ends openly, not concluded.

transcript_snippet: Every good detective story has a twist... Java Man sent science down the wrong rabbit hole for 50 years. Darwin saw it coming. Africa: case closed.
narrative_beat: The red herring — Java Man's false lead, the Out of Asia theory. Then Darwin vindicated. Then the verdict: all clues point to Africa. This is the climax.
lyria_prompt: Two-part documentary climax. Part 1 (first 60s): Thriller tension — syncopated strings, dissonant piano, sense of misdirection and confusion, 80 BPM. Part 2 (final 60s): Resolution builds — African percussion returns, strings swell, kora melody rises, momentum toward a verdict. Not triumphant fanfare — more like deep certainty arriving. 75 BPM. No pop instrumentation. No full orchestral explosion.

transcript_snippet: One story. One single shared family tree with roots planted deep in African soil. Every border, every wall — built on top of one ancient, undivided human family.
narrative_beat: The emotional close. The science resolves into a philosophical statement: all human division is a recent invention. Warm, profound, quietly powerful.
lyria_prompt: Gentle, profound resolution. Solo cello or oud melody over a soft string pad. 52 BPM, unhurried. Feels like sitting with a large truth. Warm but not sentimental — no swelling chorus, no movie-trailer crescendo. African tonal color. Fades naturally over 8-10 seconds. No percussion. No drama. Just warmth and stillness.
